_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of how AI adoption can assist developers with service-oriented design.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to evaluate and adopt new technologies, frameworks, and tools for data and AI work, assessing maturity, community support, integration requirements, and organizational fit before recommending adoption.
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI TF domain. TF-A-003 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AG-K-006 (AG, cosine: 0.570), AB-K-010 (AB, cosine: 0.566).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.5/10 (raw 0.550). Shared vocabulary: [adoption, ai]. DCWF-only terms [assist, design, developers, knowledge, oriented, service] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, adopt, assessing, community, data, evaluate] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.878 (P=0.896, R=0.861). P exceeds R by 0.036 — the DCWF element's 11 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 27 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Knowledge of how AI adoption can assist developers with service-oriented design." and "Ability to evaluate and adopt new technologies, frameworks, and tools for data a" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.586 ≈ STS 0.550 (gap 0.036). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [adoption, ai] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 5.9%. Shared tokens: [adoption, ai]. DCWF-only: [assist, design, developers, knowledge, oriented, service]. WIDAI-only: [ability, adopt, assessing, community, data, evaluate].
